title: Modi Seeks to Project Influence on Asia-Pacific Trip
gist: Modi's six-day Indonesia-Australia-New Zealand tour reasserts India as an Indo-Pacific security provider and global-south leader amid a dormant Quad and unreliable US, with an India-Indonesia missile deal and Pakistan's parallel Central Asia/Iran-mediation push.

insights.claim: India signed a deal to sell missiles to Indonesia, solidifying its emerging status as a net security provider in the Indo-Pacific, having already sold missile packages to the Philippines and Vietnam.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: India as an arms exporter to Southeast Asia is an investable defense-industrial trend and a China-balancing lever.

insights.claim: Modi is reasserting Indo-Pacific engagement at a moment when the Quad hasn't held a leaders' summit in nearly two years and the US approach under Trump is especially unclear.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: India is stepping into a leadership vacuum as US regional commitment wavers—watch India-led minilaterals.

insights.claim: Current US-India tensions mean continued US support to counter Beijing—including intelligence sharing that helped India repel Chinese border provocations—is not guaranteed, raising the value of Australia and Japan partnerships.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: US reliability risk is pushing India toward diversified security partners—a durable realignment.

insights.claim: India is generally well-liked across the Asia-Pacific (except China), a sharp contrast to rising anti-India sentiment in its own South Asian neighborhood.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: India's soft-power asymmetry favors eastward economic and security expansion over its backyard.

insights.claim: New Zealand ties are part of India's broader push into the Pacific islands, driven by competition with China and access to critical minerals.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: Critical-minerals competition is drawing India into Pacific diplomacy—a supply-chain angle.

insights.claim: Pakistani media report another round of US-Iran technical talks could be hosted in Pakistan, even as Trump declared the US-Iran cease-fire 'over' after Hormuz escalation and fresh strikes.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: Pakistan's mediation role gives it leverage with Washington that India conspicuously lacks.

insights.claim: Pakistan is deepening Central Asia engagement (a Kyrgyzstan visit, a cross-border railroad linking Pakistan-Afghanistan-Uzbekistan) in a region where India's footprint is modest because Pakistan denies India transit rights.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: Geography locks India out of resource-rich Central Asia—a structural constraint on Indian connectivity ambitions.

insights.claim: Pakistan reported nearly 10,000 new HIV infections in the first nine months of 2024, with new infections up 200 percent between 2010 and 2024 and an estimated 350,000 people living with HIV.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: A public-health deterioration signal for a fragile, populous state.

insights.claim: Sri Lanka's prisons hold about 40,000 prisoners—four times their capacity—a factor in a deadly riot that killed at least 28.
insights.speaker: Michael Kugelman
insights.so_what: Institutional overcrowding underscores governance fragility in Sri Lanka.
